label: Mailchimp
description: Connect to Mailchimp to manage audiences, campaigns, and subscriber data for email marketing.

form.title: Connect Mailchimp
form.subtitle: Paste your Mailchimp API key to give Anton access to your audiences and campaigns.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste a Mailchimp API key — it ends with a datacenter suffix like `-us21`. No OAuth setup required; takes about 30 seconds.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Mailchimp API Key

Mailchimp API keys are scoped to your account and include your datacenter in the suffix (e.g. `abc123def456-us21`). Anton uses that suffix to route requests to the right regional endpoint automatically.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Mailchimp](https://login.mailchimp.com/).
2. Click your profile icon in the bottom-left corner and select **Profile**.
3. Go to **Extras → API keys**.
4. Click **Create A Key**.
5. Optionally give it a name like `Anton` so you can identify it later.
6. Copy the full key — it ends with a `-usXX` datacenter suffix. This suffix is important; do not trim it.
7. Paste the key into the **API Key** field below.

### Notes

- Mailchimp API keys grant full account access — store them carefully.
- If you need to revoke access, return to **Extras → API keys**, find the key, and click **Invalidate**.
- The datacenter suffix (e.g. `-us21`) tells Anton which regional API server to use. Anton extracts it automatically from the key you paste.
- Read [Mailchimp's API key guide](https://mailchimp.com/help/about-api-keys/) for full background.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Your full Mailchimp API key including the `-usXX` datacenter suffix. Found under Profile → Extras → API keys.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0
methods.description: Authorize via Mailchimp's OAuth flow using your own connected app. Requires creating an app in Mailchimp's developer portal to obtain a client ID and secret.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via OAuth 2.0

Use this method if you prefer token-based authorization without exposing a long-lived API key. You'll need to register an app in Mailchimp's developer portal first.

### Steps

1. Go to the [Mailchimp Developer Portal](https://mailchimp.com/developer/) and log in.
2. Navigate to **Registered Apps → Register An App**.
3. Fill in the app name (e.g. `Anton`), description, and set the **Redirect URI** to `http://127.0.0.1` (or the local redirect Anton provides).
4. After saving, copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the app detail page.
5. Paste them into the fields below and click **Submit**.
6. A browser window will open to Mailchimp's consent screen — log in and click **Allow**.
7. Anton exchanges the authorization code for an access token and stores it locally.

### Notes

- Mailchimp OAuth tokens do not expire but can be revoked from your registered app settings.
- After authorization, Anton will call the Mailchimp metadata endpoint to determine your datacenter automatically.
- To revoke access, go to **Registered Apps** in the developer portal and delete or disconnect the app.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: From Mailchimp Developer Portal → Registered Apps → your app's Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID; found on the same registered app detail page.
